display.purpose: Hero and section headings only. Broad, bold serif.

body.purpose: All running text. Clean, unobtrusive, reliable.

label.purpose: Tracked uppercase labels, buttons, form labels.

script.purpose: Italic taglines, accent quotes, hand-lettered warmth.

shadows.name: card-hover
shadows.purpose: Ambient lift on card hover state. Never used at rest.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Gold uppercase CTA button on dark or light backgrounds.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #b78330; color: #1e2d3d; padding: 12px 28px; border-radius: 4px; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.88; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #b78330; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Service Card
components.description: Three-column service grid card with gold top border accent.
components.html:
Maintenance & Routine Service

From standard outboard maintenance to complex mechanical repairs, we keep your boat running the way it should.
components.css: .ds-card-service { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid rgba(30, 45, 61, 0.1); border-top: 3px solid #b78330; border-radius: 6px; padding: 36px 28px; transition: transform 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-card-service:hover { transform: translateY(-4px); box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(30, 45, 61, 0.1); } .ds-card-service-title { font-family: Alkaline, Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.1rem; font-weight: 700; color: #1e2d3d; margin-bottom: 12px; line-height: 1.3; } .ds-card-service-desc { font-size: 0.95rem; color: #1e2d3d; opacity: 0.75; line-height: 1.7; }

components.name: Contact Info Card
components.description: Contact method card with gold left border accent.
components.css: .ds-card-contact { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid rgba(30, 45, 61, 0.1); border-left: 4px solid #b78330; border-radius: 6px; padding: 20px 24px; transition: transform 0.2s ease; color: #1e2d3d; text-decoration: none; } .ds-card-contact:hover { transform: translateX(4px); } .ds-card-contact-icon { width: 24px; height: 24px; color: #b78330; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-card-contact-label { font-size: 0.75rem; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #b78330; font-weight: 600; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-card-contact-value { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1e2d3d; line-height: 1.5; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; }

components.name: Form Input
components.description: Text input and textarea with gold focus state.
components.css: .ds-input-label { font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1e2d3d; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-input { font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; color: #1e2d3d; background: #f4f1e4; border: 1px solid rgba(30, 45, 61, 0.2); border-radius: 4px; padding: 12px 16px; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #b78330; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #1e2d3d; opacity: 0.4; }

components.name: FAQ Accordion
components.description: Expandable FAQ item with gold open state.
components.html:
What areas do you serve?+

We serve Cape Fear, NC and the surrounding area, within roughly one hour of south Wilmington.
components.css: .ds-faq { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.05); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1); border-radius: 6px; overflow: hidden; } .ds-faq[open] { border-color: #b78330; } .ds-faq-q { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #f4f1e4; padding: 20px 24px; cursor: pointer; list-style: none; display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; transition: color 0.2s; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-faq-q::-webkit-details-marker { display: none; } .ds-faq-q:hover { color: #b78330; } .ds-faq-icon { font-size: 1.4rem; color: #b78330; transition: transform 0.2s; flex-shrink: 0; margin-left: 16px; } .ds-faq[open] .ds-faq-icon { transform: rotate(45deg); } .ds-faq-a { font-size: 0.95rem; color: #f4f1e4; opacity: 0.8; line-height: 1.8; padding: 0 24px 24px; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; }

components.name: Navbar Link
components.description: Uppercase navigation link in dark navbar.
components.html: ServicesAboutTestimonials
components.css: .ds-nav-link { color: #f4f1e4; font-size: 0.9rem; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; transition: color 0.2s; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #b78330; }

narrative.overview:
This is a design system built for a mobile marine service that shows up, knows its craft, and doesn't waste your time. It rejects the polished-corporate marine aesthetic (gold-anchor clipart, rope borders, navy blazer) in favor of something more honest: the textures and tones of an actual working waterfront — diesel-stained concrete, galvanized steel, worn brass fittings, the deep channel at dusk.

The system is flat by default, tonal by nature. Depth comes from contrast — navy fields against cream, gold against dark — not from shadows. The palette is constrained: a single saturated accent (Brass) earns its place by scarcity. Typography is muscular (Alkaline display) paired with clean system body text. There is no decoration that doesn't earn its keep.
keyCharacteristics: Dark-on-light section alternation (Channel Deep / Dock Paper) creates rhythm without cards
Single accent color (Brass) used sparingly for paths and emphasis
Uppercase tracked labels as a deliberate brand voice, not scaffolding
Flat surfaces, sharp edges, purposeful restraint
No nautical kitsch — the work speaks for itself

rules.name: The Brass Rarity Rule
rules.body: Brass appears on ≤10% of any given screen. Its scarcity is what gives it weight. If a page has more Brass than Channel Deep, the accent has become the background — pull it back.

rules.name: The No-Nautical-Kitsch Rule
rules.body: No anchor icons, rope textures, boat-wheel motifs, or anything that reads 'decorative marine.'

rules.name: The Alkaline Rule
rules.body: Alkaline is for headings only. Never use it for body copy, captions, or labels. Its weight demands breathing room.

rules.name: The Uppercase Discipline
rules.body: The tracked uppercase label is a deliberate brand element, not default section grammar. Currently used on the hero (location tagline) and FAQs only. Every other section lets the heading stand alone.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces have no shadow at rest. Elevation appears only as a response to interaction (card hover, button press).

dos: Use Brass sparingly — ≤10% of any screen. Its rarity is its power.
Alternate Channel Deep and Dock Paper as the rhythm of the page. Dark section, light section, dark, light.
Keep surfaces flat at rest. Depth comes from tonal contrast, not shadows.
Set body text at Channel Deep (#1e2d3d) on Dock Paper or White backgrounds. Verify ≥4.5:1 contrast.
Use Alkaline for headings only — it needs room to breathe.
Let the work speak. No decoration that doesn't earn its place.
donts: Don't use anchor icons, rope borders, boat-wheel motifs, or any decorative nautical kitsch.
Don't use side-stripe borders (>1px) on cards as a decorative accent — unless it's the deliberate gold/steel left/top accent system.
Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism, or bounce/elastic easing.
Don't default to editorial-magazine aesthetics (italic serif display, ruled columns, drop caps).
Don't use Weathered Tin (#7a9bb5) for body text — it fails contrast on Dock Paper.
Don't give every section a tracked uppercase eyebrow. Currently used on hero and FAQs only — any new section must earn its eyebrow.
Don't use corporate-polished or 'nautical bank' design language.
Don't ship pure black (#000) or pure gray (#808080).
Don't use bounce or elastic easing.
